preview_text:
The model favors Uzbekistan at 47%, while DR Congo's win probability stands at 23%. The underdog faces a sizeable gap of roughly 24 percentage points, positioning Uzbekistan as the clear statistical favorite on the neutral ground.

The draw emerges as the second-most likely outcome at 30%, only 17 points behind Uzbekistan's win probability. This relatively compact spread between draw and Uzbekistan victory suggests a competitive match where DR Congo cannot be ruled out entirely, though the model leans firmly toward either a Uzbekistan win or a stalemate over an upset.

A substantial divergence exists between the model and the sportsbook consensus. The market assigns DR Congo a 41% win probability—17 percentage points higher than the model—while pricing Uzbekistan's chances at 30%, some 17 points lower. Polymarket similarly favors DR Congo more than the model does, though the gap is slightly narrower there. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the disagreement flags a meaningful uncertainty worth monitoring.
